_comment: RAT catalog/v1 conformance vectors — ADR-003 shared golden data. The SINGLE source of truth for the catalog axis. Both independent references (Go: examples/catalog/inmemory-go, Python: examples/catalog/inmemory-py) load THIS file and drive CatalogService (GetTable / CreateBranch / MergeBranch) over real gRPC. Catalog owns table metadata + git-like branch/version semantics — the basis for branch-isolated pipeline runs. The references model a deterministic snapshot scheme so two impls stay in lockstep: branches are global; the reference SEEDS one table ('warehouse.sales.orders') on branch 'main' at snapshot 'snap-0'; CreateBranch copies the source branch's snapshot; MergeBranch bumps a global counter and sets the target branch to 'snap-<counter>' (first merge -> 'snap-1'). The lifecycle is STATEFUL (steps share one catalog, run in order); a step with expect.code asserts a gRPC error mid-sequence (used for the optimistic-concurrency reject after the target has moved). The MERGE-SAFETY contract (reviews/06 #8) is what's under test: optimistic concurrency (expected_into_snapshot) + idempotency (idempotency_key -> already_applied).
